fixture:        transmon_smoke.msh
kind:           REAL — DeviceLayout.jl SingleTransmon mesh (MSH 4.1 ASCII, Tet4)
sha256:         5b3ff4c357a4dc905e7a2e42abc8178778b626e47582c7bbe610f95d332b33dd
generated:      2026-07-14T00:00:00+00:00 (UTC) on the issue-#490 EC2 box
                (a working Julia + Cairo environment)
package:        DeviceLayout.jl v1.15.0 (AWS aws-cqc/DeviceLayout.jl)
julia driver:   reference/julia/generate_transmon_fixture.jl
generator note: drives the upstream example's `single_transmon()` builder
                (examples/SingleTransmon/SingleTransmon.jl) with
                `wave_ports=false`, forces first-order Tet4 + MSH 4.1 ASCII
                via `_force_first_order_msh41!()`, and writes with
                `DeviceLayout.save(out_path, sm)`.
one-line fix:   `save(out_path, sm)` → `DeviceLayout.save(out_path, sm)`
                — `save` is not exported by DeviceLayout, so the bare call
                fails to resolve; only manifested in a working Julia env.
                This is the sole delta vs the script as previously committed.

parameter values (DeviceLayout `single_transmon()` defaults, blog
optimization start [4.14, 5.591] GHz):
  geom:
    cap_length:      620 um
    cap_gap:         30 um
    total_length:    5000 um
    cpw_width:       10 um
    cpw_gap:         6 um
    readout_length:  2700 um   (wave_ports = false)
    substrate:       4 mm x 3.7 mm
  materials:
    substrate_eps_diag: 9.3, 9.3, 11.5   (sapphire, anisotropic + rotated)
    substrate_material_axes: [0.8,0.6,0.0],[-0.6,0.8,0.0],[0.0,0.0,1.0]
                          (~36.87 deg in-plane rotation → xy coupling)
    substrate_mu_diag: 0.99999975, 0.99999975, 0.99999979   (~1)
    substrate_loss_tan: 3.0e-5, 3.0e-5, 8.6e-5
    vacuum_eps: 1.0
  ports:
    port_1 / port_2:  R = 50 ohm, Direction +X
    lumped_element:   L = 14.860 nH, C = 5.5 fF, Direction +Y

physical-group schema (name → gmsh attribute/tag, from the generator log):
  NOTE: DeviceLayout lets Gmsh assign the numeric tags; the geode-fem
  adapter resolves each group by NAME, not by these numbers.
    (3, 1) substrate
    (3, 2) vacuum
    (2, 3) exterior_boundary
    (2, 4) lumped_element
    (2, 5) metal
    (2, 6) port_1
    (2, 7) port_2

physical-group element counts (adapter test assertions):
  (3, 1) substrate             64549 tets
  (3, 2) vacuum                68765 tets
  (2, 3) exterior_boundary      1732 tris
  (2, 4) lumped_element            4 tris
  (2, 5) metal                 13084 tris
  (2, 6) port_1                    4 tris
  (2, 7) port_2                    4 tris

mesh totals:
  nodes:          22684
  tets:           133314   (64549 substrate + 68765 vacuum)
  tagged tris:    14828    (all surface triangles are tagged; 0 untagged)
